title: Rising Edge Detector
description: Emits a single 1-tick pulse when a signal turns ON (rising edge). The monostable circuit: when the input goes high, a repeater-delayed copy soon cancels the initial pulse via a NOT gate, yielding exactly one brief output tick.

steps.title: Foundation
steps.explanation: 4-wide, 1-deep stone base. Signal enters from x=0 and the monostable network occupies x=1-3.

steps.title: Input lever
steps.explanation: Lever at (0,1,0). Dust at (1,1,0) fans the input. When lever turns ON, (1,1,0) goes high immediately and the pulse travels east.

steps.title: Repeater + NOT gate
steps.explanation: Repeater at (1,1,0) facing east, delay=1. Its output at (2,1,0) is used to power the anchor of a NOT torch at (2,0,0) facing east. The NOT torch output is DELAYED_NOT. Normally (lever off) the NOT torch is lit; when lever turns on, NOT torch extinguishes but the direct path momentarily still reaches the lamp before the repeater cancels it.

steps.title: Output lamp
steps.explanation: Dust at (3,1,0) receives output from the NOT torch (east face at x=2). When lever turns ON: the rising edge at the NOT input extinguishes the torch for exactly 1 tick before the repeater cancels the anchor power — producing a 1-tick high pulse at the lamp (3,0,-1).
